source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure Pod Security Policy is Enabled and set as appropriate

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure Pod Security Policy is Enabled and set as appropriate
input_row.description: Pod	Security	Policy	should	be	used	to	prevent	privileged	containers	where	possible	and enforce	namespace	and	workload	configurations.
input_row.rationale: A	Pod	Security	Policy	is	a	cluster-level	resource	that	controls	security	sensitive	aspects	of the	pod	specification.	A	PodSecurityPolicy	object	defines	a	set	of	conditions	that	a	pod must	run	with	in	order	to	be	accepted	into	the	system,	as	well	as	defaults	for	the	related fields.	When	a	request	to	create	or	update	a	Pod	does	not	meet	the	conditions	in	the	Pod Security	Policy,	that	request	is	rejected	and	an	error	is	returned.	The	Pod	Security	Policy admission	controller	validates	requests	against	available	Pod	Security	Policies. PodSecurityPolicies	specify	a	list	of	restrictions,	requirements,	and	defaults	for	Pods created	under	the	policy.	See	further	details	on	recommended	policies	in	Recommendation section	5.2. Impact: If	you	enable	the	Pod	Security	Policy	controller	without	first	defining	and	authorizing	any actual	policies,	no	users,	controllers,	or	service	accounts	can	create	or	update	Pods.	If	you are	working	with	an	existing	cluster,	you	should	define	and	authorize	policies	before enabling	the	controller.
input_row.audit: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 271	|	P a g e 2. Select	the	desired	cluster,	and	within	the	'Security	>	PodSecurityPolicies'	pane, make	sure	the	PSP	State	is	'enabled'	and	there	are	many	policies	defined.
input_row.remediation: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. Select	the	desired	cluster,	and	within	the	'Security	>	PodSecurityPolicies'	pane,	click on	PSP	State	button	to	enable	PSP. 3. Follow	the	ACK	document	to	setup	recommended	policies	in	Recommendation section	5.2: https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/173620.htm Default	Value: By	default,	Pod	Security	Policy	is	disabled. References: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/173620.htm 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/pod-security-policy Additional	Information: Pod	Security	Policy	is	Beta	in	Kubernetes	and	in	ACK.	This	feature	is	not	covered	by	any SLA	or	deprecation	policy	and	might	be	subject	to	backward-incompatible	changes. CIS	Controls: Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 5.4	Deploy	System	Configuration	Management	Tools Deploy	system	configuration	management	tools	that	will	automatically	enforce	and redeploy	configuration	settings	to	systems	at	regularly	scheduled	intervals. 272	|	P a g e
input_row.impact: If	you	enable	the	Pod	Security	Policy	controller	without	first	defining	and	authorizing	any actual	policies,	no	users,	controllers,	or	service	accounts	can	create	or	update	Pods.	If	you are	working	with	an	existing	cluster,	you	should	define	and	authorize	policies	before enabling	the	controller.
input_row.references: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/173620.htm 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/pod-security-policy Additional	Information: Pod	Security	Policy	is	Beta	in	Kubernetes	and	in	ACK.	This	feature	is	not	covered	by	any SLA	or	deprecation	policy	and	might	be	subject	to	backward-incompatible	changes. CIS	Controls: Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 5.4	Deploy	System	Configuration	Management	Tools Deploy	system	configuration	management	tools	that	will	automatically	enforce	and redeploy	configuration	settings	to	systems	at	regularly	scheduled	intervals. 272	|	P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- Pod Security Policies (PSP) are a feature of Kubernetes, and given that Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) builds upon Kubernetes, the state of PSP in a cluster can be determined using standard Kubernetes tools and APIs which are accessible via Alibaba Cloud APIs and CLI tools.
- Pod Security Policies can be programmatically checked by interacting with the Kubernetes API server, which is how these settings are managed. This suggests that an API-based check can confirm the enabled state and policy configurations.
- The audit section indicates that verification can be done through the Alibaba Cloud Console, but automated verification can be performed via Alibaba Cloud CLI or Aliyun CLI, checking the Kubernetes cluster configurations including PSP state and policies.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ali_ack_cluster_pod_security_policy_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use Alibaba Cloud CLI or Aliyun CLI’s container service commands to interact with ACK and obtain the list of configurations and policies.
- Execute commands to list the current state of Pod Security Policies within the specified Kubernetes cluster:
  - Use Kubernetes API through `kubectl` commands integrated with Alibaba Cloud (such as `kubectl get psp`) to verify the presence and active state of Pod Security Policies.
- Ensure that at least one Pod Security Policy is defined and is not in a disabled state.
- The check should capture the following configuration aspects:
  - Whether the PSP admission controller is enabled.
  - Enumeration of defined PSPs to ensure there are policies actively enforced.
- Status is compliant if the PSP controller is enabled and at least one valid Pod Security Policy is present.
- Example of automated verification checks:
  - `kubectl get psp` should return one or more policies.
  - The PSP should have appropriate restrictive settings aligned with organization security standards.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. Pod Security Policies (PSP) can indeed be checked using Kubernetes tools and APIs, which are accessible through Alibaba Cloud's CLI tools. The use of `kubectl` commands integrated with Alibaba Cloud is appropriate for this task. The Alibaba Cloud CLI and Aliyun CLI can facilitate these checks by interacting with the Kubernetes API server.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are generally accurate. The use of `kubectl get psp` is valid for checking the presence and state of Pod Security Policies. However, the description could be enhanced by explicitly mentioning the need to check if the PSP admission controller is enabled, which is a critical part of the compliance check.
- The program name `ali_ack_cluster_pod_security_policy_enabled` is somewhat descriptive but could be improved for clarity. It should reflect the specific check being performed, such as verifying the presence and configuration of PSPs.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The assessment could benefit from a more detailed explanation of how to verify that the PSP admission controller is enabled. This could include specific commands or checks within the Kubernetes API.
- Consider mentioning the use of Alibaba Cloud's SDKs or Config for additional automation capabilities or compliance checks.
- A better program name suggestion could be `alicloud_ack_psp_compliance_check` to clearly indicate that the check involves compliance with Pod Security Policies.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded in its approach and technical execution. The use of Kubernetes tools and Alibaba Cloud CLI is appropriate for automating this compliance check.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Enhance the automation details by explicitly including steps to verify that the PSP admission controller is enabled. Consider updating the program name to `alicloud_ack_psp_compliance_check` for improved clarity and descriptiveness.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that the state and configuration of Pod Security Policies (PSP) in Alibaba Cloud ACK clusters can be programmatically checked using Kubernetes APIs, which are accessible via Alibaba Cloud CLI, Aliyun CLI, or `kubectl`. The PSP resources and the admission controller status are exposed through these interfaces, making automation feasible and reliable for compliance auditing. Manual inspection is unnecessary given the robust API access.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
alicloud_ack_cluster_pod_security_policy_compliance

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Alibaba Cloud Resource/Service: Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) Clusters
- API/CLI Method:  
  - Use `kubectl get psp` to list Pod Security Policies in the cluster.
  - Use `kubectl api-versions | grep policy/v1beta1` (or equivalent) to confirm PSP API is enabled.
  - Use Alibaba Cloud CLI or Aliyun CLI to fetch cluster configuration if available.
- Properties to Check:
  - At least one PodSecurityPolicy resource exists (`kubectl get psp` returns non-empty list).
  - The PSP admission controller is enabled (PSP API version is present and active).
  - Policies are configured to restrict privileged containers and enforce namespace/workload security.
- Pass Criteria:
  - PSP admission controller is enabled.
  - At least one PodSecurityPolicy is defined.
  - Policies enforce restrictions as per CIS recommendations (e.g., privileged: false, appropriate runAsUser, etc.).
- Fail Criteria:
  - PSP admission controller is disabled.
  - No PodSecurityPolicies are defined.
  - Policies are overly permissive or missing required restrictions.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on automation. The second opinion suggested a more descriptive program name and emphasized checking the admission controller status. This feedback is incorporated in the final program name and automation details for completeness.

**SOURCE:** CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
